run.message: Don't do anything, just tell me why the original run didn't return any leads? You did quite a few searches, what didn't work.
run.assistantText:
Summary / evidence
- What I ran produced a CSV at workspace/alfred/artifacts/0e546010-f65b-4fe8-af3e-d24b3deee4ec/leads.csv, but it only contains the header row (0 leads, 0 emails). Last run id: 73318fe5-2376-46b2-b79d-8394f1d568c4. Session facts show fetchedPageCount = 0, collectedLeadCount = 0, collectedEmailCount = 0.

Most likely reasons (prioritized, with rationale and confidence)
1) No pages were fetched (High confidence ~80–95%)
   - Evidence: fetchedPageCount = 0. If the pipeline never fetched pages, there was nothing to extract from.
   - Likely causes: search provider returned no usable results, the search -> shortlist step filtered out everything, or web fetches were blocked/failed (timeouts, network or browser automation errors).

2) Search or query targeting was too narrow / mismatched to available public data (Medium–High confidence ~60–80%)
   - Reasoning: small MSPs/SIs (<50 employees) often have minimal web footprints and sometimes list only generic contact forms or role emails. If the discovery queries included strict filters (Texas + MSP/SI + company-size <50 + requirement for personal email) many matches can be excluded.

3) Email-enrichment step not run or failed / no public emails found (Medium confidence ~50–70%)
   - Evidence: collectedEmailCount = 0. Even if a company was discovered, email enrichment (email_enrich or scraping contact pages) may not have been executed or may have returned no valid emails.
   - Small businesses often only publish role-based addresses (info@, contact@) or use contact forms, which our pipeline may treat as unacceptable if configured to prefer personal/professional addresses.

4) Extraction / quality gates rejected candidates (Medium confidence ~40–70%)
   - The pipeline may have strict minConfidence, verification or deduplication rules (for example: require verified email format, company domain match, role match). If those thresholds were high, borderline candidates could be dropped.

5) Bot blocks, JS-heavy sites or robots.txt prevented scraping (Lower–Medium confidence ~30–60%)
   - Some MSPs use single-page apps or block automation; that can prevent content parsing even when the site exists. This aligns with zero fetched pages if fetches failed due to these protections.

6) Host-diversity / shortlist heuristics pruned too aggressively (Low–Medium confidence ~20–50%)
   - If the shortlist step intentionally enforces host diversity or removes low-quality hosts, and there were only a few low-quality hits, the shortlist might be empty.

7) Intermittent provider or network errors (Unknown confidence)
   - Search provider outages, rate limits, or transient network problems could cause an otherwise-valid run to return nothing. I didn’t run diagnostics, so I can’t say for sure.

Why I think the top cause is fetch/search failure
- The direct fact fetchedPageCount = 0 points to discovery/fetch as the choke point. Without fetched pages you can't extract leads or emails, so everything downstream is blocked.

Concrete next steps (pick one or tell me which to do; I will not run anything unless you ask)
- Run diagnostics on the last run (recommended) — run_diagnostics and/or search_status to confirm whether the search provider or fetch stage failed. Tradeoff: quick definitive answer vs you asked not to run anything now.
- Re-run discovery with relaxed filters: allow role-based emails, increase maxPages, or relax company-size filter (e.g., <100 instead of <50). Tradeoff: higher yield but possibly lower precision.
- Run an email-enrichment pass on any previously discovered domains (if any exist) — only useful if fetch succeeded previously.
- Provide seed domains/names you already have so I can enrich them instead of re-discovering.

If you want my prioritized recommendation without me running anything now
1) If you prefer diagnostics first: run run_diagnostics and search_status to confirm search/fetch health. 2) If you want results fast: re-run with defaults I suggested earlier (Owner/C

availableAgents.name: lead_agent
availableAgents.description: Specialist agent for lead generation and enrichment workflows.

availableAgents.name: research_agent
availableAgents.description: Specialist for web research, synthesis, and drafting.

availableTools.name: doc_qa
availableTools.description: Answer questions from local documentation/files with citations and bounded context.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for repo/docs questions before coding; pass scopePaths to narrow search.

availableTools.name: email_enrich
availableTools.description: Enrich current lead state with business emails from website/contact pages.
availableTools.inputHint: Use after lead discovery to improve email coverage; can run with or without browser fallback.

availableTools.name: file_edit
availableTools.description: Perform text replacement edits in an existing file.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for precise in-file edits when a direct find/replace is sufficient.

availableTools.name: file_list
availableTools.description: List files/directories inside the project root for navigation and planning edits.
availableTools.inputHint: Use before reading/editing files to discover relevant paths safely.

availableTools.name: file_read
availableTools.description: Read text file content from the project root with bounded slices.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for precise context gathering before making edits.

availableTools.name: file_write
availableTools.description: Write or append text content to a file inside the project root.
availableTools.inputHint: Use to create/update files after planning; keep writes scoped and reversible.

availableTools.name: lead_extract
availableTools.description: Extract structured leads from already-fetched pages stored in the current agent state.
availableTools.inputHint: Use after web_fetch when you want an atomic fetch -> extract flow instead of the full lead_pipeline.

availableTools.name: lead_pipeline
availableTools.description: Run the lead extraction pipeline over search + browse + extraction + quality gate.
availableTools.inputHint: Use when you need more validated leads. Increase maxPages for deeper crawl or tune minConfidence for stricter/looser gating.

availableTools.name: lead_search_shortlist
availableTools.description: Search and keep only fetch-worthy URLs before browsing, with objective-aware scoring (domain/content/geo/email signals) and host diversity.
availableTools.inputHint: Use before web_fetch when you want a lighter search -> shortlist -> fetch -> extract flow.

availableTools.name: process_list
availableTools.description: List local processes for diagnostics and lifecycle control.
availableTools.inputHint: Use with query filters before attempting process_stop.

availableTools.name: process_stop
availableTools.description: Stop a local process by PID (trusted mode only).
availableTools.inputHint: Use carefully after confirming target with process_list.

availableTools.name: recover_search
availableTools.description: Attempt recovery of the primary search provider (for example restarting local SearXNG).
availableTools.inputHint: Use when search provider appears down; then re-check status and retry search/lead_pipeline.

availableTools.name: run_diagnostics
availableTools.description: Analyze run timeline/tool telemetry and summarize likely failure points with recommendations.
availableTools.inputHint: Use after poor runs to pinpoint failure sources before retrying.

availableTools.name: search
availableTools.description: Run provider-backed web search and return top results.
availableTools.inputHint: Use to test/expand discovery queries before running deeper lead extraction.

availableTools.name: search_status
availableTools.description: Check live health of search providers and whether primary recovery is available.
availableTools.inputHint: Use when search-yield is low or search failures indicate provider outages/timeouts.

availableTools.name: shell_exec
availableTools.description: Execute a shell command in the project workspace (trusted mode only).
availableTools.inputHint: Use for diagnostics and local workflow commands. Avoid destructive operations.

availableTools.name: web_fetch
availableTools.description: Fetch and parse web pages using browser automation from query results or explicit URL list.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for deterministic page retrieval before extraction. Prefer maxPages <= 12 unless necessary.

availableTools.name: write_csv
availableTools.description: Write current accumulated leads to artifact CSV.
availableTools.inputHint: Use at the end of the run (or checkpoints) to persist current lead set.

availableTools.name: writer_agent
availableTools.description: Generate structured written drafts (blog/email/memo/etc.) from instructions and optional local context.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for drafting content quickly; set outputPath to save draft to a file.
